Defines structures and functions for drawing menu cell layers in a user interface.

# Purpose
The `menu_layer.h` file is a C header file that defines the interface for drawing menu cells in a graphical user interface. It provides a set of functions and data structures to configure and render menu items with titles, subtitles, icons, and various alignment options. The file includes definitions for `MenuCellLayerConfig`, which allows customization of menu cell appearance, including fonts, icon alignment, and text overflow behavior. The file also defines several constants for cell dimensions and insets, particularly for round displays.

The header file includes functions for drawing different types of menu cells, such as [`menu_cell_layer_draw`](<#menu_cell_layer_draw>), [`menu_cell_basic_draw`](<#menu_cell_basic_draw>), and [`menu_cell_title_draw`](<#menu_cell_title_draw>), each offering different layout options for titles, subtitles, and icons. It also provides utility functions like [`menu_cell_layer_is_highlighted`](<#menu_cell_layer_is_highlighted>) to check the highlight status of a cell and functions to retrieve default cell dimensions. The file is part of a larger user interface module, as indicated by the inclusion of other headers like `layer.h` and `applib/graphics/text.h`, and it is intended to be used in conjunction with a menu layer callback system for rendering menu items in a structured manner.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `layer.h`
- `applib/fonts/fonts.h`
- `applib/graphics/text.h`
- `stdint.h`
- `stddef.h`


# Data Structures

---
### MenuCellLayerIconAlign
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - ``MenuCellLayerIconAlign_Left``: Aligns the icon to the left using `GAlignLeft`.
    - ``MenuCellLayerIconAlign_Right``: Aligns the icon to the right using `GAlignRight`.
    - ``MenuCellLayerIconAlign_TopLeft``: Aligns the icon to the top-left using `GAlignTopLeft`.
    - ``MenuCellLayerIconAlign_Top``: Aligns the icon to the top using `GAlignTop` (only if `PBL_ROUND` is defined).
- **Description**: Defines alignment options for icons in a menu cell layer, allowing icons to be positioned to the left, right, top-left, or top (conditional on `PBL_ROUND`).


---
### MenuCellLayerConfig
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``title``: Pointer to a string for the cell's title.
    - ``subtitle``: Pointer to a string for the cell's subtitle.
    - ``value``: Pointer to a string for the cell's value.
    - ``title_font``: Font used for the title text.
    - ``value_font``: Font used for the value text.
    - ``subtitle_font``: Font used for the subtitle text.
    - ``overflow_mode``: Text overflow mode for the cell.
    - ``icon``: Pointer to a bitmap for the cell's icon.
    - ``icon_align``: Alignment of the icon within the cell.
    - ``icon_box_model``: Pointer to a box model for the icon's layout.
    - ``icon_form_fit``: Boolean indicating if the icon should fit the form.
    - ``horizontal_inset``: Horizontal inset for the cell layout.
- **Description**: Defines the configuration for a menu cell layer, including text, fonts, icon, and layout properties. It allows customization of the cell's appearance by specifying titles, subtitles, values, and their respective fonts. The structure also includes settings for icon alignment and layout, as well as text overflow handling and horizontal insets.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### menu\_cell\_layer\_draw<!-- {{#callable_declaration:menu_cell_layer_draw}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_cell_layer.h#L67>)

Draws a menu cell layer using the specified configuration.
- **Description**: Use this function to render a menu cell layer with a given configuration. It must be called within a drawing context, typically inside a `.draw_row` callback. Ensure that the `cell_layer` size is sufficient to accommodate all specified cell items, as items may not be drawn if the layer is too small. This function does not handle invalid input parameters, so ensure all inputs are valid before calling.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: The graphics context where the cell layer will be drawn. Must not be null.
    - `cell_layer`: The layer representing the cell to draw. Must not be null.
    - `config`: A pointer to a `MenuCellLayerConfig` structure containing the configuration for the cell layer. Must not be null.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`menu_cell_layer_draw`](<menu_layer_system_cells.c.md#menu_cell_layer_draw>)  (Implementation)


---
### menu\_cell\_basic\_draw\_icon\_right<!-- {{#callable_declaration:menu_cell_basic_draw_icon_right}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_cell_layer.h#L101>)

Draws a menu cell with a title, subtitle, and an icon on the right.
- **Description**: Use this function to draw a menu cell with a title, subtitle, and an optional icon aligned to the right. Call it within the `.draw_row` callback implementation to render a cell in a menu layer. If the `cell_layer` size is insufficient to fit all elements, some may not be drawn. Ensure that the graphics context and cell layer are valid before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: The destination graphics context. Must be a valid `GContext` pointer.
    - `cell_layer`: The layer of the cell to draw. Must be a valid `Layer` pointer.
    - `title`: The title text to draw. If non-null, it appears in larger text (24 points, bold Raster Gothic system font). Can be null.
    - `subtitle`: The subtitle text to draw. If non-null, it appears in smaller text (18 points, Raster Gothic system font). If null, the title is centered vertically.
    - `icon`: The icon to draw on the right of the text. If non-null, it is displayed; if null, the space is used for the title and subtitle.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`menu_cell_basic_draw_icon_right`](<menu_layer_system_cells.c.md#menu_cell_basic_draw_icon_right>)  (Implementation)


---
### menu\_cell\_basic\_draw\_custom<!-- {{#callable_declaration:menu_cell_basic_draw_custom}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_cell_layer.h#L117>)

Draws a custom menu cell with specified fonts and layout options.
- **Description**: Use this function to draw a menu cell with customizable fonts for the title, value, and subtitle, as well as an optional icon. This function is useful when you need more control over the appearance of a menu cell beyond the default styles. It must be called within a drawing context, typically inside a `.draw_row` callback. Ensure that the `cell_layer` is large enough to accommodate all elements, as items may not be drawn if space is insufficient.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: The graphics context where the cell will be drawn. Must not be null.
    - `cell_layer`: The layer representing the cell to draw. Must not be null.
    - `title_font`: The font to use for the title text. Must be a valid `GFont`.
    - `title`: The title text to display. Can be null if no title is needed.
    - `value_font`: The font to use for the value text. Must be a valid `GFont`.
    - `value`: The value text to display. Can be null if no value is needed.
    - `subtitle_font`: The font to use for the subtitle text. Must be a valid `GFont`.
    - `subtitle`: The subtitle text to display. Can be null if no subtitle is needed.
    - `icon`: The icon to display in the cell. Can be null if no icon is needed.
    - `icon_on_right`: A boolean indicating whether the icon should be drawn on the right side of the cell. If false, the icon is drawn on the left.
    - `overflow_mode`: Specifies how text should behave when it overflows its bounding box. Must be a valid `GTextOverflowMode`.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`menu_cell_basic_draw_custom`](<menu_layer_system_cells.c.md#menu_cell_basic_draw_custom>)  (Implementation)


---
### menu\_cell\_small\_cell\_height<!-- {{#callable_declaration:menu_cell_small_cell_height}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_cell_layer.h#L161>)

Returns the height of a small menu cell.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the pixel height of a small menu cell, which is useful for layout calculations in user interfaces. This function does not require any parameters and can be called at any time to retrieve the current default small cell height for the runtime platform.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns an `int16_t` representing the height of a small menu cell in pixels.
- **See Also**: [`menu_cell_small_cell_height`](<menu_layer_system_cells.c.md#menu_cell_small_cell_height>)  (Implementation)


---
### menu\_cell\_basic\_horizontal\_inset<!-- {{#callable_declaration:menu_cell_basic_horizontal_inset}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_cell_layer.h#L170>)

Returns the default horizontal inset for a menu cell.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the default horizontal inset value for a menu cell, which is useful for layout calculations in user interfaces. This function does not require any parameters and can be called at any time to retrieve the current default inset value.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns an `int16_t` representing the default horizontal inset in pixels for a menu cell.
- **See Also**: [`menu_cell_basic_horizontal_inset`](<menu_layer_system_cells.c.md#menu_cell_basic_horizontal_inset>)  (Implementation)
